title: watercolor meadow
description: Soft watercolor washes with bleeding edges, gaussian-jittered blobs, and paper grain texture.
notes: Each 'wash' is built from many overlapping semi-transparent ellipses with gaussian-random position jitter — the same technique real watercolor artists get from wet paper bleeding. Three wash passes per terrain layer with slightly varied colors create that characteristic depth. Paper grain is pixel-level noise applied twice (before and after painting). Trees are clusters of green blobs; flowers are bezier stems with bloom blobs at the tip.
